setting.note: The provincial León of the early Somoza years, mapped through a sensational poisoning trial: a compact colonial grid of cathedral and plaza, courthouse and pharmacy, the grand houses of the local elite and the railway station that ties the town to the coast. Ramírez maps a real city, and the map keeps to its streets.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Sergio Ramírez, Castigo divino, first published 1988. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Divine Punishment, Sergio Ramírez (1988). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The oxblood red of León's tiled roofs and the case's blood-and-poison undertow. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
